problems.title: Two Sum Indices
problems.prompt: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to target. You may assume exactly one solution exists, and you may not use the same element twice. Return the indices in any order.

problems.title: Contains Duplicate
problems.prompt: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice, and false if every element is distinct.

problems.title: Isomorphic Strings
problems.prompt: Given two strings s and t, determine if they are isomorphic. Two strings are isomorphic if characters in s can be replaced to get t. Each character must map to exactly one character, and no two characters may map to the same character (while preserving order).
constraints: s and t consist of any valid ASCII character

problems.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.prompt: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the total number of contiguous subarrays whose sum equals k.

solution.python:
from collections import defaultdict

class Solution:
    def subarraySum(self, nums: list[int], k: int) -> int:
        prefix_count: dict[int, int] = defaultdict(int)
        prefix_count[0] = 1
        total = answer = 0
        for num in nums:
            total += num
            answer += prefix_count[total - k]
            prefix_count[total] += 1
        return answer

problems.title: Top K Frequent
problems.prompt: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
constraints: k is in the range [1, number of unique elements]
The answer is guaranteed to be unique

problems.title: Longest Consecutive
problems.prompt: Given an unsorted array of integers nums, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. You must write an algorithm that runs in O(n) time.

problems.title: Design HashMap Lite
problems.prompt: Design a HashMap without using built-in hash-table libraries. Implement MyHashMap with put(key, value), get(key) (return -1 if absent), and remove(key). Keys and values are non-negative integers.
constraints: At most 10^4 calls to put, get, and remove

samples.input: put(1,1); put(2,2); get(1); get(3); put(2,1); get(2); remove(2); get(2)

problems.title: Find All Anagrams
problems.prompt: Given two strings s and p, return an array of all starting indices of p's anagrams in s. You may return the answer in any order. An anagram is a permutation of all characters.
constraints: s and p consist of lowercase English letters
